sutta: The Buddha said truthfully monks these are to be reckoned as gains forest dwelling living on alms wearing rag robes wearing three robes only teaching the Dhamma mastery of the Vinaya wide knowledge the rank of an elder thera the blessing of true development the blessing of a following the blessing of a large following being a man of good family a beautiful complexion pleasant speech to be content with little and freedom from sickness.
commentary: So here the Buddha is saying these things are really worth gaining these are good things to have to practice I would give you a lot of blessing the first one is forest dwelling this is basically for mangala the word forest is actually a translation of the word I think arania aranya is basically a secluded place a quiet place and usually the Buddha mentions a place like a cave up in the fall up in the hills um very quiet secluded place basically because if a monk wants to practice well it is best to to to live in such places the second one also for monks is living on arms that means to go on arms round this is a very good practice for monks because it is not something easy to do sometimes you go on arms around people are quite rude to you they just ask you to shovel or something like that and or they just ignore you or they give you a roots there or something and uh it's uh very good for our ego if we want to wear away our ego these are some of the things which are very helpful that's why it's not easy to be among some people they don't realize that and they I don't appreciate monks enough and that's a loss to them third one is wearing rag ropes rag ropes here is a translation for uh this rope this this the meaning of it now is that it is picked up a cloth that is picked up during the Buddha's time according to the later books uh something like uh 20 or 25 years the first 20 or 25 years of the Buddha's ministry ministry uh he and his disciples only wore uh robes that were made from uh a cloth that was picked up and they would go to these what they call channel grounds cemeteries places where dead bodies were thrown and um the cloth that was left over and the mouse would go and pick it up or from whatever place the cloth was thrown away they would pick it up and sow them into a rope but later the lay disciples of the Buddha asked the Buddha for permission to give ropes to the monks and out of compassion for them to give people a chance to cultivate marina the Buddha allowed uh lay people to make offerings to uh monks uh sometimes uh ap some people don't uh don't realize that it is uh the good fortune to be able to give to monks and uh that is uh that is the the Buddha's so this about red robes and the third one the fourth one is wearing three ropes only the ropes of a manga are generally stated to be three one is the sabung or one sometimes it's called the sarong and then this rope that we are wearing there's a shivarra shivon and then there's another one that's two-layer double lid that is for use as a blanket now then teaching Dhamma teaching Dhamma is also a good thing the Buddha encouraged the teaching of Dhamma that's why the Buddha you find during the Buddha's time and up to now that the Buddha encouraged his monks to teach the Dhamma and not all monks taught Dhamma and not all monks taught meditation there were some monks who were meditation monks and some monks who were dharma preachers and some monks who mastered both and during the Buddha's time there were many types of monks and the Buddha allowed all of them to do what was they what they thought was best for them and the mastery of the Vinaya uh another one also concerning monks is mastery of the rules and the disciplinary rules and if they could live by the disciplinary code that would accumulate they would accumulate a lot of blessings the next one is wide knowledge white knowledge is a translation for bahu sacha that means a lot of knowledge of the dharma the Buddha said now very few people can teach the very few people can teach the Dhamma according to the the real uh essence the real Dhamma that the Buddha taught then the next one is the rank of an elder an elder here is a monk is one who has 10 vasas and above a man who has 10 vasas and above is a man who can ordain new monks among who has 10 vassals and above can accept disciples the Buddha did not allow a younger monk to accept disciples but the Buddha asked the monks to practice for the first 10 years to get a lot of experience then after that only to accept disciples that's why uh to be an elder is a blessing the blessing of true department that means a man who can conduct himself well and that is uh a good blessing then the blessing of a following that means uh if a monk can have a following of disciples that that is a blessing um this following can be lay disciples or among disciples and during the Buddha's time we find that several monks they had a large following of among disciples and nowadays we find that not many monks are able to have a following of among disciples some of them have a following of lay disciples but to be had to have a following of among disciples it is very good especially for the religion because there is always a shortage of monks the blessing of a large following to have a a big following of lay disciples and among disciples during the Buddha's time he sometimes he would walk around with 1250 monks and you go from place to place and that would be really impressive and the Buddha why did the Buddha take such a large following with him it was not that I mean it's really very difficult to look after such a large amount of monks and to travel in the group it's a lot of it's like an army moving logistics involved to look after their food and all that but uh that is is it's not easy to have a large following because unless that person has enough mettā and uh enough ability to teach there would not be many monks who would want to follow him a man of good family that means to be born into a good family a good family is not necessarily a rich family good family is one which is upright who knows the Dhamma who follows the the Dhamma straightforward simple people it's not easy to find sometimes a flare a fair complexion fair complexion I think you'll understand pleasant speech to talk uh pleasantly this is one of the qualities that endear dear person to others if a person speaks very rudely unpleasant speech nobody likes to be near him or her the Buddha said all of us are born with an axe in our tongue in the dharmapatha here if you're not careful we simply swing the x and we hurt ourselves to be content with little uh somebody who has few a few wishes a few things to ask for and freedom from sickness this is another great blessing to be seldom signal now the next two ties.
